india.name: India
india.description: The world's largest democracy. Citizens elect 543 Members of Parliament (MPs) to the Lok Sabha. The party or coalition with majority forms the government, and the Prime Minister is the head of government.

timeline.description: ECI announces elections; strict conduct rules kick in for all parties and candidates.
Final electoral rolls published. Citizens can verify/correct their names at local booths.
Candidates file nominations with Returning Officers. Deposits required.
Returning Officers verify candidate eligibility and documents.
Last day for candidates to withdraw their nominations.
Active campaigning: rallies, advertisements, door-to-door outreach.
48-hour silent period before polling. No new campaigning allowed.
Citizens vote at designated booths using Electronic Voting Machines (EVMs).
Votes counted under strict supervision. Results declared constituency-wise.
Winning party/coalition invited to form government; PM sworn in.

steps.title: Register as a Voter
steps.detail: Be 18+, Indian citizen. Register at voters.eci.gov.in or local BLO (Booth Level Officer).

steps.title: Verify Your Name
steps.detail: Check electoral roll at your local booth or online using your EPIC number.

steps.title: Know Your Constituency
steps.detail: Find your Lok Sabha / Vidhan Sabha constituency using the Voter Helpline App.

steps.title: Collect Voter ID / Slip
steps.detail: Carry your EPIC card or any approved photo ID on polling day.

steps.title: Cast Your Vote
steps.detail: Visit your assigned booth, get inked finger, press EVM button for your candidate.

steps.title: Results & Government Formation
steps.detail: Party crossing 272 seats forms government; President invites to form cabinet.

facts: India uses Electronic Voting Machines (EVMs) since 1999
Voter turnout in 2024 General Election: ~67%
Polling can span 7 phases across 44+ days
NOTA (None of the Above) option available since 2013
543 seats in Lok Sabha, elected every 5 years
ECI uses a multi-phase approach to ensure transparency

usa.name: United States
usa.frequency: Presidential every 4 years; Congress every 2 years
usa.description: Citizens vote for President via the Electoral College (538 electors). Congress consists of the Senate (100 seats) and House of Representatives (435 seats). Presidential candidates need 270 electoral votes to win.

timeline.description: Party primaries and caucuses held state-by-state to select party nominees.
Democrats and Republicans formally nominate their presidential candidates.
Presidential debates broadcast nationally; VP debate included.
Many states open early in-person voting or mail ballot distribution.
Most states require registration 15–30 days before Election Day.
First Tuesday after first Monday in November. Polls open 6am–8pm local time.
Electors in each state officially cast their Electoral College votes.
Congress certifies the Electoral College results in joint session.
January 20th: new President sworn in at the U.S. Capitol.

steps.title: Register to Vote
steps.detail: Register at vote.gov. Deadlines vary by state (15–30 days before election).

steps.title: Find Your Polling Place
steps.detail: Use vote.gov or your county clerk's website to find your assigned polling location.

steps.title: Check ID Requirements
steps.detail: ID laws vary by state — some require photo ID, others accept signature matching.

steps.title: Consider Mail-In Voting
steps.detail: Request absentee/mail ballot early. Available in all 50 states with varying rules.

steps.title: Cast Your Vote
steps.detail: Vote for President, Congress, and local races on paper ballots or touchscreen machines.

steps.title: Electoral College Decides
steps.detail: 270 of 538 electoral votes needed to win the presidency. Results certified by Congress.

facts: The US has no federal holiday for Election Day
Electoral College was established in 1787
2020 saw record 158.4 million votes cast
Some states allow same-day voter registration
538 total Electoral College votes needed
Winner-take-all system in 48 states and DC

uk.name: United Kingdom
uk.frequency: Every 5 years (Fixed-term) or earlier by PM
uk.description: Citizens elect 650 Members of Parliament to the House of Commons using First Past the Post. The leader of the party with most MPs is invited by the Monarch to become Prime Minister and form a government.

timeline.description: Parliament dissolved; all MPs' terms end. Writ of election issued.
Candidates submit nomination papers and £500 deposit to returning officers.
Last day to register to vote or change registration details.
Final date to apply for a postal vote.
Candidates and parties campaign. Strict spending limits apply.
Always a Thursday. Polls open 7am–10pm. Paper ballots, X marks the box.
Counts begin election night. Most seats declared within 24 hours.
Winning party leader visits Buckingham Palace; PM commissioned by the Monarch.

steps.title: Register to Vote
steps.detail: Register at gov.uk/register-to-vote. Must be 18+ and a UK/qualifying citizen.

steps.title: Choose Voting Method
steps.detail: Vote in person, by post, or by proxy. Apply for postal/proxy vote in advance.

steps.title: Know Your Constituency
steps.detail: 650 constituencies each elect one MP. Find yours at election.parliament.uk.

steps.title: Bring Voter ID
steps.detail: Photo ID now required at polling stations (introduced 2023). Check accepted forms.

steps.title: Mark Your Ballot
steps.detail: Receive ballot, enter booth, place a single X next to your chosen candidate.

steps.title: Government Formation
steps.detail: Party with majority invited by King to form government; PM enters Downing Street.

facts: UK uses First Past the Post — no proportional representation
Polling day is always a Thursday by convention
Candidates lose deposit if they get less than 5% of votes
The House of Lords is unelected — appointed or hereditary peers
650 constituencies, each electing one MP
Fixed 5-year term unless early dissolution

eu.name: European Union
eu.description: Citizens of 27 EU member states elect 720 Members of the European Parliament (MEPs). Each country uses its own electoral system (mostly proportional). The Parliament co-legislates EU law alongside the Council of the EU.

timeline.description: Political parties and pan-European groups register campaigns with national authorities.
Parties publish their candidate lists for each member state.
EU citizens living abroad can vote in country of residence or home country.
Campaigns held simultaneously across all 27 member states.
4-day voting window (Thu–Sun) across all member states. Dates vary by country.
Provisional results announced Sunday evening after last polls close.
MEPs meet for first time; elect President of the European Parliament.
European Council nominates Commission President; Parliament votes to approve.

steps.title: Check Eligibility
steps.detail: Any EU citizen aged 18+ (16 in some states) can vote in country of residence or origin.

steps.title: Register in Your Country
steps.detail: EU citizens abroad must register to vote in their country of residence to avoid double-voting.

steps.title: Understand MEP Allocation
steps.detail: Seats allocated by population. Germany gets 96; Malta gets 6. Degressive proportionality.

steps.title: Vote by Party List
steps.detail: Most countries use proportional list systems. Vote for a party, not individual candidates.

steps.title: Watch Results
steps.detail: Results tallied separately per country but seats totalled across all 27 states.

steps.title: Parliament Forms Groups
steps.detail: MEPs join political groups (not national parties) — EPP, S&D, Renew, Greens, etc.

facts: 720 MEPs represent 27 countries in the European Parliament
Voting age is 16 in Austria, Belgium, Germany, Malta
EU Parliament elections use proportional representation in all states
Voter turnout hit 50.7% in 2024 — highest since 1994
Degressive proportionality ensures smaller states have fair representation
MEPs sit in political groups, not national delegations

brazil.name: Brazil
brazil.frequency: Presidential every 4 years; Congress every 4 years
brazil.description: Brazil has compulsory voting for citizens aged 18–70. The President is elected via two-round majority system. Brazil uses fully electronic voting — one of the world's most advanced systems — since 1996.

timeline.description: Parties hold conventions to formally choose presidential and congressional candidates.
Official campaign period begins. TV/radio airtime allocated by TSE rules.
Voters must be registered 151 days before election day.
October election. If no candidate gets 50%+1, top two proceed to runoff.
Runoff between top two candidates — 4 weeks after first round.
President takes office on January 1st following election year.

steps.title: Obtain Voter Title (Título)
steps.detail: Register with your Regional Electoral Court (TRE). Mandatory for ages 18–70.

steps.title: Find Your Voting Section
steps.detail: Your exact voting location (seção eleitoral) is on your voter title card.

steps.title: Use the Electronic Ballot
steps.detail: Enter candidate number on the urna eletrônica. Confirm with green button.

steps.title: Two Rounds if Needed
steps.detail: If no majority in Round 1, return 4 weeks later for the runoff.

steps.title: Compulsory Voting
steps.detail: Voting is mandatory. Missing without justification requires payment of a small fine.

steps.title: Justify if Absent
steps.detail: Travelers/ill voters can justify absence at TRE within 60 days after election.

facts: Voting is compulsory for ages 18–70 (optional for 16–17 and 70+)
Brazil's electronic voting system has been used since 1996
TSE allocates free TV/radio campaign time by party size
A valid voter title is required to get a passport or public job
Two-round system ensures majority winner
One of the world's most advanced electronic voting systems
